profile.name: Di Wang
profile.passion: Personalized Intelligence & Model Serving on Any Body

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Figure AI
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: General-purpose humanoid robot, backed by OpenAI/Microsoft

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Groq
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: LPU inference chips, fastest LLM serving

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Rabbit
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: LAM (Large Action Model) for personal AI

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Modular/Mojo
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Next-gen AI programming language and runtime

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Cerebras
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Public (CBRS on Nasdaq; IPO priced May 13, 2026 at $185/share; $5.55B raised; $56.4B fully diluted valuation; began trading May 14, 2026 with early prints implying ~$100B market cap)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: WSE-3 wafer-scale chip; $510M 2025 revenue; $10B OpenAI compute deal; priced at $185 (well above $150-160 revised range); raised $5.55B — largest AI chip IPO in history; Day 1 trading (May 14) showed early prints near $350/share (~$100B implied market cap); validates pure-play AI infrastructure as public market category; NVIDIA alternatives now investor-credible at scale

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Baseten
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Serverless ML model deployment; $150M Series D Apr 2026; abstracts GPU fleet for enterprise inference workloads; competes with Modal Labs, Parasail

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Cursor
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Series C (in talks, $2B raise at $50B+ valuation, Apr 2026)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: AI-native code editor with 400K+ paying developers; in talks to raise $2B led by a16z + NVIDIA at $50B+ valuation (Apr 19, 2026); signals developer tools reaching infrastructure-tier valuations; direct competitor to GitHub Copilot and new agent coding frameworks

enterpriseWatchlist.name: MatX
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Ex-Google TPU founders; $500M Series B led by Jane Street + Situational Awareness (Leopold Aschenbrenner); targeting 10x LLM training perf over H100; TSMC-fabbed, first shipments 2027; credible non-NVIDIA training chip challenger

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Lambda Labs
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: 'Superintelligence Cloud' positioning; $2.5B+ total capital deployed to build gigawatt-scale AI factories (10K Blackwell Ultra GPUs in KC facility); J.P. Morgan-led $1B credit facility announced May 7, 2026; competes with CoreWeave (CRWV, IPO'd) for GPU-native cloud market below hyperscalers

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Project Prometheus
enterpriseWatchlist.domain: Physical AI for Industrial Manufacturing Simulation
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: $10B round at $38B valuation (closed Apr 2026, JPMorgan + BlackRock co-led)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Co-CEOs Jeff Bezos + Vik Bajaj; builds AI simulation models to automate chip fabs, automotive assembly, and aerospace manufacturing; launched with $6.2B, added $10B round at $38B val in Apr 2026; Bezos also targeting $100B acquisition fund for industrial companies; bifurcates from 'embodied robotics' into factory process simulation — $16T manufacturing TAM with <2% AI penetration

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Sunday Robotics
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Building 'Memo', a household robot entering beta later in 2026; $165M Series B at $1.15B valuation — first consumer robotics unicorn in this category; targets sub-$5K price point; competes with labor costs (cleaning services, elder care); Mountain View, CA. Category signal: if Memo ships, it validates consumer robotics as mass market (analogous to Roomba → iPhone step change)
